title: Teucrium embergeri
taxonomy: Teucrium embergeri is classified within the family Lamiaceae. This family, commonly known as the mint family, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Teucrium embergeri is further classified as a species within the genus Teucrium, which comprises approximately 300 species of shrubs and small herbs. The genus Teucrium is a part of the order Lamiales, which also includes other families such as Boraginaceae and Oleaceae. Teucrium embergeri is native to the island of New Caledonia.

intro: Teucrium embergeri is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as germander, and is native to New Caledonia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of cognitive function, and potential protection against neurodegenerative diseases. The bioactive constituents of Teucrium embergeri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support brain health and cognitive function.
